reachability_artifacts: integration test running the real loop command processor against two SQLite connections, with command, claim, effect, and operation-event rows captured
database migration transcript showing a pre-operations supervisor fixture opened and queried after migration

effort_complexity.rationale: See spec.json; command taxonomy, transaction boundaries, and safe event projection require design review.

release_benefit: A SwornAgent operator can trust a recoverable autonomous release loop, receive durable attention pages, and monitor or safely control it from a mobile web board.

positive_scenarios: A control intent is accepted once and its lifecycle is queryable after restart.
Concurrent processors grant only one operation claim.
negative_scenarios: Duplicate submissions cannot duplicate an operation.
Unsafe event detail cannot reach durable storage.
validation.benefit_hypothesis: A durable command/event authority gives every adapter and recovery path the same auditable operation semantics.

design_decisions.choice: Store commands, operations, and events in the existing repository-local SQLite database.
design_decisions.rationale: The existing database already coordinates cross-process loop ownership and can make command acceptance atomic.

design_decisions.choice: Make notification and UI data consume typed public-safe operation events.
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified 2026-07-14 (D4): hosted, self-hosted, UI, and notification projections consume typed public-safe events from the local authority rather than reading or mutating repository state directly.
design_decisions.rationale: One safe projection prevents later transports from parsing logs or exposing internal payloads.
